title: Lightly Fermented Indian Lotus Root Curry
description: A tangy, bouncy curry made from fermented lotus root, fresh spinach, and tofu, simmered on a salt-block for a lazy Sunday lunch.
instructions: Pat the lotus root rings dry and place them in a bowl with 1 tbsp salt for fermentation; cover and let sit at room temperature for 12 hours.
Remove the lotus root from the brine, rinse, and pat dry.
Preheat a salt block pan over medium heat and lightly oil it with olive oil.
Sauté onion, garlic, and ginger on the salt block until translucent.
Add cumin seeds and let them pop for 30 seconds.
Stir in turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, and tomatoes; cook until tomatoes soften.
Add the fermented lotus root rings and tofu cubes to the pot, stirring to coat with spices.
Pour in water, season with 1 tsp salt, reduce heat, and simmer for 20 minutes until lotus root is tender.
Stir in fresh spinach and cook until wilted, about 2 minutes.
Transfer the curry to a serving dish, garnish with chopped cilantro, and serve warm with a side of whole30-friendly flatbread if desired.
